key_insight: INCARBench shows that LLMs can often produce plausible VASP INCAR settings, but truly correct scientific configurations remain much harder—especially when parameters are physically coupled.
engineering_relevance: For computational materials workflows, this means LLMs may help draft or repair inputs, but expert validation is still essential before running expensive simulations.
method: Built INCARBench to evaluate LLMs on VASP INCAR configuration generation and repair.
Tested 19 model configurations across semantic accuracy, policy accuracy, and task-critical correctness.
Analyzed failure modes in challenging settings such as DFT+U, magnetism, and correlated materials.
evidence_signal: Across 19 model configurations, several achieved high semantic and policy accuracy, yet task-critical correctness was substantially lower, and repair tasks exposed weak configuration-preservation ability.
watch_item: A major concern is that superficially valid parameter sets can still violate physical constraints, so automated outputs should not be trusted without domain-specific checks.
paper_title: INCARBench: A Benchmark for Scientific Configuration in VASP INCAR by Large Language Models
